verses.text: The LORD: speak to all the congregation of Israel — ye shall be holy, for I the LORD your God am holy. Guidance: The banner of the chapter. Everything below — gleaning fields, paying wages, weighing flour — comes from this one root. God is holy; you reflect Him in every ordinary thing. Holiness is the daily weather of His people.

The LORD: speak to all the congregation of Israel — ye shall be holy, for I the LORD your God am holy. Guidance: The banner of the chapter. Everything below — gleaning fields, paying wages, weighing flour — comes from this one root. God is holy; you reflect Him in every ordinary thing. Holiness is the daily weather of His people.

Every one fear his mother and father; keep My sabbaths — I am the LORD. Turn not to idols nor make molten gods. Peace offering — eat day of sacrifice and the next; the third day burn. Eaten the third day — abomination, not accepted, cut off. Guidance: Holiness begins at home — honor parents, keep sabbath, reject idols. Then it shapes worship: even your joy meals have a clock. Don't let yesterday's celebration spoil into today's irreverence.

When ye reap the harvest, reap not wholly the corners; gather not the gleanings. Glean not thy vineyard, neither gather every grape; leave them for the poor and the stranger. I am the LORD your God. Guidance: God writes welfare into the harvest. Leave corners and fallen grapes — the poor will come pick them. Ruth lived in this verse. Generosity is not a special offering; it is what the field looks like.

Steal not, deal not falsely, lie not one to another. Swear not by My name falsely; neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD. Guidance: Three commandments compressed: don't take, don't lie, don't drag God's name into a lie. Honesty in pocket, in mouth, in oath. The LORD's name belongs to truth — never to cover-up.

Steal not, deal not falsely, lie not one to another. Swear not by My name falsely; neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the LORD. Guidance: Three commandments compressed: don't take, don't lie, don't drag God's name into a lie. Honesty in pocket, in mouth, in oath. The LORD's name belongs to truth — never to cover-up.

Defraud not thy neighbor; rob not. Wages of the hired servant not abide all night until morning. Curse not the deaf; put no stumbling block before the blind; fear thy God. Guidance: Pay the laborer same day — overnight is wage theft. Don't curse those who cannot hear; don't trip those who cannot see. God watches what no one else watches; fear of Him is the brake when no one is looking.

Do no unrighteousness in judgment: respect not the poor, nor honor the mighty. Walk not as a talebearer; stand not against thy neighbor's blood. Hate not thy brother; rebuke him. Avenge not, bear no grudge; love thy neighbor as thyself: I am the LORD. Guidance: Three sins of the tongue — partial judgment, gossip, false witness. Three of the heart — hatred, vengeance, grudge. Then one summary: love thy neighbor as thyself. Jesus quoted this as the second greatest commandment.

Keep My statutes. Cattle shall not mate with a diverse kind; thy field not sown with mingled seed; thy garment not of linen and woolen together. Guidance: God built creation with distinctions. Mixing kinds blurs what He set apart. The rules feel small; the principle is large — when boundaries blur, identity blurs too.

If a man lie with a bondmaid betrothed but not yet redeemed — they shall be scourged, not put to death; she was not free. He brings a trespass offering ram to the priest. Priest atones with the ram; forgiven. Guidance: A complicated case — a betrothed slave woman, neither free nor married yet. God's law protects her from death (which married adultery would require) and still demands restitution. Even unclear cases get clear answers.

When ye come into the land and plant trees for food, count the fruit uncircumcised three years — not eaten. Fourth year all fruit holy, praise to the LORD. Fifth year ye shall eat. Guidance: A four-year wait for fruit. The first three you don't even try; the fourth you give all to God; the fifth you finally taste. Patience is built into the orchard. God's people learn slowness from the soil.

Eat not anything with blood. Use not enchantment, nor observe times. Round not the corners of your head, mar not the corners of your beard. Cuttings for the dead not on flesh, print no marks: I am the LORD. Guidance: Three pagan practices — divination, ritual shaving, mourning cuts and tattoos. All used the body to court spirits or appease them. God reserves the body's surface for Himself. Skin belongs to its Maker.

Prostitute not thy daughter — lest the land fall to whoredom and become full of wickedness. Keep My sabbaths; reverence My sanctuary: I am the LORD. Regard not familiar spirits, nor seek wizards — defile not yourselves: I am the LORD your God. Guidance: Three lines protect three goods: daughters' bodies, the holy day, and the soul that might wander to spirit-mediums. God's people guard what He gave — flesh, time, and trust.

Rise up before the grey-haired, honor the face of the old man, and fear thy God: I am the LORD. Guidance: Stand when an elder enters. The body's posture teaches the heart its place. Honor for the aged is not just sentiment — it's a fear of the God who counts every silver hair.

If a stranger sojourn with thee, vex him not. The stranger shall be unto you as one born among you; love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in Egypt: I am the LORD your God. Guidance: The same word that bound Israel together — love thy neighbor as thyself — extends to the foreigner. Memory of slavery becomes the engine of welcome. You were strangers; never forget it.

If a stranger sojourn with thee, vex him not. The stranger shall be unto you as one born among you; love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in Egypt: I am the LORD your God. Guidance: The same word that bound Israel together — love thy neighbor as thyself — extends to the foreigner. Memory of slavery becomes the engine of welcome. You were strangers; never forget it.

Do no unrighteousness in measures — meteyard, weight, measure. Just balances, just weights, just ephah, just hin — I am the LORD who brought you out of Egypt. Observe all My statutes — I am the LORD. Guidance: The chapter closes at the marketplace. Honest weights are holiness in the produce aisle. Same God who set Israel free now wants His freed people to weigh out grain fairly. Holiness has a scale.
